response:
The fare calculation follows a clear rule where spring brings new hope for travelers. If the distance is four kilometers or less then the cost is fixed at ten yuan. When the trip goes beyond four kilometers the driver adds two yuan for every extra kilometer. Fractions of a kilometer are always rounded up to the nearest whole number for billing. This means the total fare depends directly on the rounded distance value. The spring season often sees more tourists visiting the airport and hotels.

A passenger paid thirty-eight yuan for a trip that covered fifteen kilometers of actual driving. We must calculate the extra distance caused by stopping time to find the maximum wait. The base fare is ten yuan so the extra cost is twenty-eight yuan. Dividing twenty-eight by two gives us fourteen extra kilometers that were charged. Since the actual distance was fifteen kilometers the rounded distance must be nineteen kilometers. The difference between nineteen and fifteen is four kilometers of added distance. Each kilometer of added distance equals five minutes of stopping time. Therefore the taxi stopped for at most twenty minutes during this journey.
